original: Bright Water’s external toxin pump hasn’t yet been given a name, and you can see why. The bulbous central cylinder hangs from a harness of industrial straps, meant to be looped around the wearer’s torso. Two support tubes, glowing neon green, stretch out to a pair of metallic silver cuffs. The inner edge, where they would press against skin, is lined with a strangely undulating white leather or hyper-dense fabric, you’re not sure which.\n\nThe attached development notes indicate that the system can painlessly extract toxins from the wearer’s blood without the need for needles or an intravenous connector. A later note indicates a desire to reduce the efficacy of the system, so as not to negatively impact sales of Bright Water’s pharmaceutical products.

Feruze wings down from a perch above as you approach the taxi, hefting a dufflebag up over her shoulder. “So? You ready to get this party started?” You nod, checking your own [pc.weapon][pc.cl mercenary |. Feruze grins toothily. |, though hopefully you won’t have cause to use it. ] “Alright, finally!” the big shark cheers, waving down one of the passing hover-cabs.[flags.FERUZE_CAME_INSIDE |“We’re gonna shake that little [rival.mf |bastard |bitch ] down for our kids, right?” You nod. “Absolutely.” |“Fuck, can’t believe that little shit’s making us go through all this effort. My rep’s gonna take a beating if I lose a Steele.” [flags.RIVAL_DANCE |“Yeah, I’m worried too...” |You grunt noncommittally. ] ] A taxi finally descends down to your level, side hatch popping open before it’s even stopped moving. A pair of clearly-intoxicated, giggling zaika stagger out flashing you chrome-framed grins and winking cybernetic eyes. Feruze all but shoves them out of the way and piles into the back seat. Guess that leaves you for the front. A much less augmented zaika is sitting in the driver’s seat, and when you hand her the credential chip, she whistles. “Whew, fancy pants on this offworlder! You sure you wanna go down there, though? Last I heard there’s a pretty big riot fixing to break out in the Farrow.” “A riot?” “Mmhm! Well, m’wife insists it’s a protest for the sake of the planet’s future, but the [targetFactionName] radio ain’t mincing words. Announcer says there’s a whole mess of anti-capital rebels trying to tear down the very fabric of our society for their misguided ideals. How awful.” You glance back at Feruze. “Mind turning that radio on while we fly down?” “Sure thing, spacer!” the cabby says, lifting off and fiddling with her console. You’re assaulted by a crackling of static, and then a sultry-smooth voice comes over the speakers. “-are continuing to block traffic into and around [targetFactionName]’s most important and profitable facilities in the Farrow Industrial Zone, preventing hundreds of workers from benefiting from their high-paying and benefit-laden jobs within. Our illustrious leader has already promised a swift end to these obstructions at any cost, but the class traitors have made it known that they will not be moved until their craven demands are met. We contacted the facility overseer for comment:” Another voice comes over the wire, rough as a gravel tumbler, though still doubtless a zaika. “A work week of less than sixty hours is frankly absurd and threatens the entire productivity cycle all of us break our backs to maintain. These selfish bitches can’t see the bigger picture — we’re playing catch up with the whole galaxy down here now. There’s no time for slacking off or crying about the cost to the environment. The sunward side burns away all the refuse from this facility anyway.” “And there you have it, darlings,” the announcers says. “Traitorous workers — and I use the term loosely — are threatening not only [targetFactionName] employee wages, but the standing of our entire world in the wider galaxy. Let’s all hope the security services will teach these thugs a lesson they’ll feel from their tips to their tails.” “Yeah, get ‘em!” the cabby cheers, punching the air. “Fuckin’ up my whole route, those assholes. You’re the first people wanting to go down there in days!” In her taxi, maybe. You glance out the window, watching the descent from the urban streets to the smoke-blanketed industrial zone below the Gyre. It’s just below, though, but also further afield than you’ve been before: your adventures so far have kept to the narrow band in the middle of the world-belting city, cozy in the perpetual twilight between the sun-scorched and frozen sides of the world. The taxi takes you sunward, far enough that you can see a golden haze glaring down even through the industrial smog. Even inside the sealed can of the transport, you can feel the heat starting to chew through the air con. Your driver fiddles with a dial, cranking it up, but it can’t keep pace with the sun’s fury as you near the hot side of the planet.

You note that her knuckles are bloodied, and there’s scraps of fabric still clinging to her teeth. “Well? You two lovebirds ready or what?”\n\t\n\t“Yep, you two can go on in,” Nilekke says. She snaps a crisp salute and marches off, rejoining the guards at the gate. By the time you’ve secured you [pc.weapon], she’s already barking orders at her troops again like it’s business as usual.

There’s a roaring cheer from the crowd as the last of the [targetFactionName] goons fall. You barely have time to stow your [pc.weapon] before a horde of zaika workers are charging the gates, cutting into them with torches and crowbars. While they work, the chrome-armed ‘leader’ of the rioters approaches with a grin. “Thanks, offworlder. Turns out all of you aliens being just as greed-mad as the overseers was another piece of propaganda. I know of an underground deprogramming camp — we’ll take these ladies there and get all this corpo shit out of their brains. Half of it’s probably literal programming in all that cyberwear.” You nod, watching some of the rioters carrying off the unconscious bodies of your enemies. A few short moments later, and then gates collapse off their hinges, slamming into the ground of the inner courtyard with a resounding thud. “Charge!” the leader yells into her bullhorn, waving the crowd through the breach. A mass of zaika bodies surge past you, storming the building — or they would, if the only entrance wasn’t a single revolving door only big enough for two of them to get through at a time. The panels must be reinforced, as several resounding blows from bats and chains don’t so much as crack them. They can’t even push the doors faster than their automated rotation allows. There’s a great deal of yelling and jostling at the front, while other rioters fan out and start looting the guard house and smaller out-buildings. Feruze sighs. “Look at that traffic jam. Guess we aren’t getting in the front door.” Even as the rioters are doing their thing, though, you glance up to see a caravan of hover-trucks passing overhead towards the back of the complex — either too self-assured or too clueless to realize the facility’s under siege. You and Feruze exchange a glance. “Cargo bay?” “Cargo bay.”
